zh.lifeSummary: 當Nicolas II Bailleul de Conde於大約1230年出生在法國諾爾-帕-德-卡拉斯諾爾德孔代-素-利斯考特時，他的父親Seigneur Jacques de Condé de Morialme21歲，母親Agnès de Roeulx27歲。他於1261年與Catherine De Cayeux結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1293年6月23日在法國皮卡迪索姆坎布隆享年64歲，埋葬在法國皮卡迪索姆坎布隆。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Nicolas II Bailleul de Conde于大约1230年出生在法国諾爾-帕-德-卡拉斯諾爾德孔代-素-利斯考特时，他的父亲Seigneur Jacques de Condé de Morialme21岁，母亲Agnès de Roeulx27岁。他于1261年与Catherine De Cayeux结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他去世于1293年6月23日在法国皮卡迪索姆坎布隆享年64岁，埋葬在法国皮卡迪索姆坎布隆。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Conde
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Manuel, Luis, Juan, Pedro, Raul, Jorge, Ruben, Angel, Cesar, Fernando.  Portuguese Goncalo, Manoel.
Spanish and Portuguese: nickname from the title of rank conde ‘count’, a derivative of Latin comes, comitis ‘companion’. See Conte .
English (Shropshire): variant of Cundy , a cognate of 3 below, or Cound, a habitational name from Cound in Shropshire.

variants.name: Conte
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Mario, Angelo, Salvatore, Antonio, Rocco, Carmine, Pasquale, Luigi, Silvio, Silverio, Giuseppe, Sal, Alfonso, Biagio, Carlo, Emilio, Fernando, Gerardo.

Italian: from the title of rank conte ‘count’, from Latin comes, genitive comitis ‘companion’. Probably in this sense (and in the Late Latin sense of ‘traveling companion’), it was a medieval personal name. As a title it was no doubt applied ironically as a nickname for someone with airs and graces or simply as a metonymic occupational name for someone in the service of a count. Compare Lo Conte .
Spanish: variant of   Conde   ‘count’ (see also 1 above).

variants.name: Candee
meanings: Americanized form of French Condé (see Conde ) or, alternatively, Candé, a habitational name from Candé in Maine-et-Loire, or from Candé-sur-Beauvron in Loire-et-Cher, or southern French Condy .
Alternatively, perhaps an altered form of English Condy , a cognate of the name Candé in 1 above.

History: The Candees trace their origin to Zaccheus Cande(e), who in the middle of the 17th century resided in the New Haven County, CT, and his probable father John Condy alias Conde. The name of the latter is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors as Jean/John Candée/Cande/Condé and in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America as John Conde. See also   Conde  .

nameMeanings.name: Nicolas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Late Greek. See Nicholas .

variants.name: Nicholas
meanings: form of the post-classical Greek personal name Nikolaos, derived from nikē ‘victory’ + laos ‘people’. The spelling with -ch- first occurred as early as the 12th century, and became firmly established at the time of the Reformation, although Nicolas is still occasionally found. St Nicholas was a 4th-century bishop of Myra in Lycia, about whom virtually nothing factual is known, although a vast body of legend grew up around him, and he became the patron saint of Greece and of Russia, as well as of children, sailors, merchants, and pawnbrokers. His feast day is 6 December , and among the many roles which legend has assigned to him is that of bringer of Christmas presents, in the guise of ‘Santa Claus’ (an alteration of the Dutch form of his name, Sinterklaas).

variants.name: Nicolao
meanings: form of the post-classical Greek personal name Nikolaos, derived from nikē ‘victory’ + laos ‘people’. The spelling with -ch- first occurred as early as the 12th century, and became firmly established at the time of the Reformation, although Nicolas is still occasionally found. St Nicholas was a 4th-century bishop of Myra in Lycia, about whom virtually nothing factual is known, although a vast body of legend grew up around him, and he became the patron saint of Greece and of Russia, as well as of children, sailors, merchants, and pawnbrokers. His feast day is 6 December , and among the many roles which legend has assigned to him is that of bringer of Christmas presents, in the guise of ‘Santa Claus’ (an alteration of the Dutch form of his name, Sinterklaas).

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Violet  ,   Vivien  , or   Vivian .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Violet
meanings: From the name of the flower (Old French violette, Late Latin violetta, a diminutive of viola). This was one of the earliest flower names to become popular in Britain, being used as early as 1700 and becoming well established in the 19th century, although it is now somewhat out of favour.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

en.lifeSummary: When Nicolas II Bailleul de Conde was born about 1230, in Condé-sur-l'Escaut, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, France, his father, Seigneur Jacques de Condé de Morialme, was 21 and his mother, Agnès de Roeulx, was 27. He married Catherine De Cayeux in 1261. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 3 daughters. He died on 23 June 1293, in Cambron, Somme, Picardie, France, at the age of 64, and was buried in Cambron, Somme, Picardie, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Nicolas II Bailleul de Conde est né environ 1230, à Condé-sur-l'Escaut, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, France, son père, Seigneur Jacques de Condé de Morialme, avait 21 ans et sa mère, Agnès de Roeulx, avait 27 ans. Il a épousé Catherine De Cayeux en 1261. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé le 23 juin 1293, à Cambron, Somme, Picardie, France, à l’âge de 64 ans, et a été enterré à Cambron, Somme, Picardie, France.

de.lifeSummary: Als Nicolas II Bailleul de Conde um 1230 in Condé-sur-l'Escaut, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Seigneur Jacques de Condé de Morialme 21 und seine Mutter Agnès de Roeulx 27. Er hat 1261 Catherine De Cayeux geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 3 Töchter. Er starb am 23. Juni 1293 im Alter von 64 Jahren in Cambron, Somme, Picardie, Frankreich und wurde in Cambron, Somme, Picardie, Frankreich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Nicolas II Bailleul de Conde è nato il circa 1230 nel Condé-sur-l'Escaut, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, Francia, il suo padre, Seigneur Jacques de Condé de Morialme, aveva 21 anni e il suo madre, Agnès de Roeulx, aveva 27 anni. Lui sposato Catherine De Cayeux nel 1261. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 3 figlie. Morì il 23 giugno 1293, nel Cambron, Somme, Picardie, Francia, all'età di 64 anni, e fu sepolto a Cambron, Somme, Picardie, Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: Nicolas II Bailleul de Condeが約1230年にフランスノード-プアス-デ-カレイスノードコンドe-スル-ル'エスカウトで生まれた時，父Seigneur Jacques de Condé de Morialmeは21歳，母Agnès de Roeulxは27歳であった。1261年にCatherine De Cayeuxと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1293年6月23日にフランスピカーディソンメカンブロンで64歳で死去し，フランスピカーディソンメカンブロンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Nicolas II Bailleul de Conde(이)가 대략 1230년 프랑스 노르드-파스-데-캘레이 노르드 콘드e-수-'스카트에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Seigneur Jacques de Condé de Morialme(은)는 21세였고, 그의 어머니 Agnès de Roeulx(은)는 27세였다. 그는 1261년 Catherine De Cayeux(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1293년 6월 23일 프랑스 피카디 솜 캄브런에서 64세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 피카디 솜 캄브런에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Nicolas II Bailleul de Conde nasceu aproximadamente 1230, em Condé-sur-l'Escaut, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, França, seu pai, Seigneur Jacques de Condé de Morialme, tinha 21 anos e sua mãe, Agnès de Roeulx, tinha 27 anos. Ele casou-se com Catherine De Cayeux em 1261. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 3 filhas. Ele faleceu em 23 de junho de 1293, em Cambron, Somme, Picardie, França, com 64 anos, e foi sepultado em Cambron, Somme, Picardie, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Nicolas II Bailleul de Conde родился около 1230 г. в местности кондe-сур-л'ескаут, норд, норд-пас-де-калеи, Франция, его отцу по имени Seigneur Jacques de Condé de Morialme было 21 год, а матери, которую звали Agnès de Roeulx, 27. Он женился на женщине по имени Catherine De Cayeux в 1261 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 3 дочери. Он умер 23 июня 1293 г. в местности камброн, сомм, пикарди, Франция в возрасте 64 лет и был похоронен в местности камброн, сомм, пикарди, Франция.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Nicolas II Bailleul de Conde nació aproximadamente 1230, en Condé-sur-l'Escaut, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, Francia, su papá, Seigneur Jacques de Condé de Morialme, tenía 21 años y su mamá, Agnès de Roeulx, tenía 27 años. Se casó con Catherine De Cayeux en 1261. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 3 hijas. Murió el 23 de junio de 1293, en Cambron, Somme, Picardie, Francia, a la edad de 64 años, y fue sepultado en Cambron, Somme, Picardie, Francia.
